FR-01.title: Customer Registration and Login
requirements: Register with phone number and OTP verification
Login via phone OTP or email/password
Social login with Google
Profile with name, email, and saved addresses

FR-02.title: Restaurant Browsing
requirements: Display nearby restaurants based on user location
Filter by cuisine, rating, delivery time, and price range
Sort by distance, rating, or popularity
Show restaurant details: menu, rating, delivery time, minimum order

FR-03.title: Menu and Item Selection
requirements: Display restaurant menu with categories
Each item shows name, description, price, and image
Items can have customization options (size, toppings)
Add items to cart with quantity selection

FR-04.title: Shopping Cart and Checkout
requirements: Cart shows selected items with quantities and prices
Users can modify quantities or remove items
Apply promo codes for discounts
Select delivery address and payment method

FR-05.title: Real-time Order Tracking
requirements: Live map showing delivery driver location
Order status updates: confirmed, preparing, picked up, delivered
Estimated delivery time with real-time updates
Push notifications for status changes

FR-06.title: Payment Processing
requirements: Support credit/debit card payments
Cash on delivery option
Digital wallet integration
Payment receipt generated for each order

FR-07.title: Rating and Review
requirements: Rate restaurant with 1-5 stars after delivery
Rate delivery driver separately
Write optional text review
View restaurant ratings before ordering

FR-08.title: Restaurant Owner Dashboard
requirements: Restaurant owners can manage menu items
View and accept/reject incoming orders
Update restaurant hours and availability
View sales analytics and revenue reports

FR-09.title: Delivery Driver App
requirements: Driver receives order assignment notifications
Navigation to restaurant and customer address
Driver can mark order as picked up and delivered
Earnings tracking and daily summary

FR-10.title: Order History
requirements: View past orders with details and status
Reorder from previous orders with one tap
Download invoice for each order
Filter orders by date range

FR-11.title: Favorites and Saved Restaurants
requirements: Users can save favorite restaurants
Quick access to frequently ordered items
Favorites list accessible from profile
Suggested restaurants based on order history

FR-12.title: Customer Support Chat
requirements: In-app chat for order issues
Chat with customer support agent
Attach images of incorrect orders
Chat history saved for reference

FR-01.title: Plan Customer Registration and Login Workflow and Contracts
FR-01.description: Review the SRS requirement bullets for customer registration and login and break them into executable API, UI, data, security, and reporting subtasks. Define the workflow states, user roles, field-level validations, request/response contracts, and failure cases before implementation starts. Use these requirement drivers as the baseline: Register with phone number and OTP verification; Login via phone OTP or email/password; Social login with Google.
acceptance_criteria: The end-to-end workflow for customer registration and login is documented from trigger to completion
Validation rules, role restrictions, and error scenarios for customer registration and login are explicitly defined
Implementation subtasks for customer registration and login can be picked up independently by engineering

FR-01.title: Implement Customer Registration and Login Backend Services
FR-01.description: Build the backend services for customer registration and login on Flutter, Node.js, PostgreSQL, Redis. Create route handlers or service endpoints, model the core business operations, enforce validation and authorization rules, persist workflow state changes, and add structured error handling and audit logging. Make sure the implementation covers these behaviors: Register with phone number and OTP verification; Login via phone OTP or email/password; Social login with Google.
acceptance_criteria: Core customer registration and login business rules are implemented for Food Delivery Application
Validation, authorization, and error handling are covered for customer registration and login
API/service behavior for customer registration and login is testable and documented for integration

FR-01.title: Implement Customer Registration and Login Frontend Flow
FR-01.description: Build the frontend flow for customer registration and login with the exact forms, tables, filters, wizards, status indicators, and action buttons required by the SRS. Connect the UI to the backend contract, handle optimistic updates or refresh flows where needed, and provide user-facing validation, permissions feedback, loading states, and recovery states.
acceptance_criteria: The customer registration and login user flow is accessible and responsive across supported browsers
Forms, filters, tables, and feedback states for customer registration and login behave correctly
The customer registration and login UI is fully integrated with backend APIs and handles loading and error states

FR-01.title: Design Customer Registration and Login Persistence Model
FR-01.description: Design the persistence layer for customer registration and login. Identify the core entities, relationships, indexes, status fields, history/audit tables, and reporting attributes required to support the workflow. Add migrations and seed or fixture data where that helps engineering validate the feature early.
acceptance_criteria: The persistence model for customer registration and login supports required workflows and reporting needs
Indexes, constraints, and relationships for customer registration and login are defined correctly
Migrations for customer registration and login can be applied safely in target environments

FR-01.title: Integrate External Dependencies for Customer Registration and Login
FR-01.description: Implement the external integration points used by customer registration and login, such as third-party APIs, SSO, messaging gateways, payment rails, import/export pipelines, or bank feeds. Add retry-safe behavior, timeout handling, observability, and fallback behavior for downstream failures.
acceptance_criteria: External dependencies used by customer registration and login are connected through stable integration layers
Timeout, retry, and failure handling for customer registration and login integrations are implemented and testable
Secrets, credentials, and integration logs for customer registration and login are handled securely

FR-01.title: Write Customer Registration and Login Automated and End-to-End Tests
FR-01.description: Write unit, integration, and end-to-end tests for customer registration and login. Cover valid flows, invalid inputs, permission failures, state transitions, data integrity checks, and regression-prone edge cases pulled from the SRS. Add fixtures or mocks that make the feature verifiable in CI and local development.
acceptance_criteria: Happy path and failure cases for customer registration and login are covered by automated tests
Permission checks and validation rules for customer registration and login are verified
Regression coverage exists for the most critical customer registration and login scenarios

FR-10.title: Build Order History Reporting and KPI Logic
FR-10.description: Implement the reporting layer for order history, including aggregate queries, KPI calculations, filters, drill-down behavior, and export-ready payloads. Ensure data shown in dashboards or reports can be traced back to transactional records and remains performant at realistic dataset sizes.
acceptance_criteria: KPI and reporting calculations for order history return accurate results
Filters, grouping, and export-ready structures for order history work correctly
Reporting queries for order history perform within acceptable limits

FR-10.title: Write Order History Automated and End-to-End Tests
FR-10.description: Write unit, integration, and end-to-end tests for order history. Cover valid flows, invalid inputs, permission failures, state transitions, data integrity checks, and regression-prone edge cases pulled from the SRS. Add fixtures or mocks that make the feature verifiable in CI and local development.
acceptance_criteria: Happy path and failure cases for order history are covered by automated tests

FR-11.title: Plan Favorites and Saved Restaurants Workflow and Contracts
